description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a robust, dark metal receiver and barrel, complemented by polished wooden furniture. The weapon features a prominent round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver, characteristic of early 20th-century designs. The stock is made of wood, shaped for ergonomic shoulder support, and the foregrip is also wooden, contoured for a secure grip. The barrel is long and cylindrical, with a simple muzzle and no visible attachments or cooling fins. Iron sights are present, with a rear notch and a front post, both fixed to the top of the receiver and barrel. The overall design is utilitarian, blending metal and wood for durability and classic aesthetics.
description: A vintage submachine gun with a dark metal body, wooden stock and foregrip, and a large round drum magazine. It features fixed iron sights and a long cylindrical barrel.
description_view_0: The machine gun is seen from the front, showing the circular face of the drum magazine and the cylindrical barrel extending forward.
description_view_1: A full side profile reveals the wooden stock, foregrip, and the large drum magazine beneath the receiver, along with the iron sights and barrel.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the back of the wooden stock and the rear of the drum magazine, with the barrel extending away from the viewer.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the same features as view 1, with the wooden stock, foregrip, drum magazine, and barrel visible from the other side.
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with ergonomic contour
